g.text: Zaba505/embedded

g.text: Three separate supplies; only the grounds are common

g.text: Star ground at PS3(-): three legs, and no other ground path in the system

g.text: The load rail is dedicated - neither board supplies the light

g.text: SUPPLY SIZING

g.desc: SUPPLY SIZING

g.text: PS3, the load supply, is the only one this design constrains:

g.desc: PS3, the load supply, is the only one this design constrains:

g.text: load design ceiling 1.0 A at 5 V, i.e. 5 W

g.desc: load design ceiling 1.0 A at 5 V, i.e. 5 W

g.text: supply rating >= 2 A, twice the ceiling

g.desc: supply rating >= 2 A, twice the ceiling

g.text: Q1 conduction loss about 25 mW at 1 A, at the 3.05 V gate drive

g.desc: Q1 conduction loss about 25 mW at 1 A, at the 3.05 V gate drive

g.text: C1 / C2 220 uF + 100 nF across the rail, at Q1

g.desc: C1 / C2 220 uF + 100 nF across the rail, at Q1

g.text: C1 supplies the switching edge locally so the inrush does not have to come

g.desc: C1 supplies the switching edge locally so the inrush does not have to come

g.text: down the supply leads; C2 handles the high-frequency content that C1's ESR

g.desc: down the supply leads; C2 handles the high-frequency content that C1's ESR

g.text: and lead inductance cannot. Neither is optional once the supply leads are

g.desc: and lead inductance cannot. Neither is optional once the supply leads are

g.text: longer than a hand's width.

g.desc: longer than a hand's width.

g.text: PS1 and PS2 are simply what their boards already require - nothing in this

g.desc: PS1 and PS2 are simply what their boards already require - nothing in this

g.text: design adds to either. 3.3 V is not supplied by anything here: both boards

g.desc: design adds to either. 3.3 V is not supplied by anything here: both boards

g.text: make their own from their own 5 V input, which is why +3V3_DUE and +3V3_PI

g.desc: make their own from their own 5 V input, which is why +3V3_DUE and +3V3_PI

g.text: are separate nets on these sheets. They are separate regulators, and tying

g.desc: are separate nets on these sheets. They are separate regulators, and tying

g.text: them together would be the same mistake as tying the 5 V rails.

g.desc: them together would be the same mistake as tying the 5 V rails.

g.text: Total draw on either 3.3 V rail is under 5 mA - two pull-ups and, on the Pi,

g.desc: Total draw on either 3.3 V rail is under 5 mA - two pull-ups and, on the Pi,

g.text: one status LED.

g.desc: one status LED.

g.text: WHERE THE GROUNDS MEET, AND WHY THERE

g.desc: WHERE THE GROUNDS MEET, AND WHY THERE

g.text: One node: the negative terminal of PS3, the load supply. Exactly three legs

g.desc: One node: the negative terminal of PS3, the load supply. Exactly three legs

g.text: leave it, and there is no other ground connection anywhere in the system.

g.desc: leave it, and there is no other ground connection anywhere in the system.

g.text: leg 1 PS3(-) -> Q1 source sheet 3 up to 1 A

g.desc: leg 1 PS3(-) -> Q1 source sheet 3 up to 1 A

g.text: leg 2 PS3(-) -> Due GND, LHS pos 18 sheet 3 microamps

g.desc: leg 2 PS3(-) -> Due GND, LHS pos 18 sheet 3 microamps

g.text: leg 3 PS3(-) -> Pi GND, J8 pin 14 sheet 5 microamps

g.desc: leg 3 PS3(-) -> Pi GND, J8 pin 14 sheet 5 microamps

g.text: PS1(-) and PS2(-) are not legs. Each supply's return is inside its own power

g.desc: PS1(-) and PS2(-) are not legs. Each supply's return is inside its own power

g.text: cable and is that board's ground by construction; they are drawn here so the

g.desc: cable and is that board's ground by construction; they are drawn here so the

g.text: diagram is complete, not because they are additional paths between subsystems.

g.desc: diagram is complete, not because they are additional paths between subsystems.

g.text: WHY PS3(-) AND NOT, SAY, THE DUE'S GROUND. That node is where the only large

g.desc: WHY PS3(-) AND NOT, SAY, THE DUE'S GROUND. That node is where the only large

g.text: current in the system already terminates. Star anywhere else and the 1 A load

g.desc: current in the system already terminates. Star anywhere else and the 1 A load

g.text: return has to travel through a length of wire that something else is using as

g.desc: return has to travel through a length of wire that something else is using as

g.text: its 0 V reference.

g.desc: its 0 V reference.

g.text: How much does that actually cost? Q1 switches in about 1 us (a 1 kohm gate

g.desc: How much does that actually cost? Q1 switches in about 1 us (a 1 kohm gate

g.text: resistor into 1077 pF of Ciss), so di/dt is roughly 1 A/us. A 300 mm jumper is

g.desc: resistor into 1077 pF of Ciss), so di/dt is roughly 1 A/us. A 300 mm jumper is

g.text: around 0.3 uH. Share it between the load return and the UART reference and

g.desc: around 0.3 uH. Share it between the load return and the UART reference and

g.text: V = L di/dt = 0.3 uH x 1 A/us = 0.3 V

g.desc: V = L di/dt = 0.3 uH x 1 A/us = 0.3 V

g.text: of ground bounce lands on a link whose low-level threshold is 0.9 V at the Pi.

g.desc: of ground bounce lands on a link whose low-level threshold is 0.9 V at the Pi.

g.text: A third of the noise margin, from wiring alone. The star costs one jumper.

g.desc: A third of the noise margin, from wiring alone. The star costs one jumper.

g.text: RULES

g.desc: RULES

g.text: * No ground wire anywhere else. In particular the Due-to-Pi UART harness

g.desc: * No ground wire anywhere else. In particular the Due-to-Pi UART harness

g.text: carries TX and RX only (sheet 6); a ground wire there closes a loop with

g.desc: carries TX and RX only (sheet 6); a ground wire there closes a loop with

g.text: legs 2 and 3, and the loop it closes encircles the load return.

g.desc: legs 2 and 3, and the loop it closes encircles the load return.

g.text: * Never omit leg 2. Q1's gate voltage is measured against its source. With

g.desc: * Never omit leg 2. Q1's gate voltage is measured against its source. With

g.text: no shared reference, VGS is undefined and the MOSFET's state is a guess.

g.desc: no shared reference, VGS is undefined and the MOSFET's state is a guess.

g.text: * Give the load return its own wire. Do not run it along a breadboard rail

g.desc: * Give the load return its own wire. Do not run it along a breadboard rail

g.text: that anything else is also calling ground.

g.desc: that anything else is also calling ground.

g.text: THE THREE 5 V RAILS ARE SEPARATE - DO NOT BRIDGE THEM

g.desc: THE THREE 5 V RAILS ARE SEPARATE - DO NOT BRIDGE THEM

g.text: PS1 -> the Raspberry Pi only 5 V, >= 2.5 A official supply

g.desc: PS1 -> the Raspberry Pi only 5 V, >= 2.5 A official supply

g.text: PS2 -> the Arduino Due only USB Programming port, or 7-12 V on VIN

g.desc: PS2 -> the Arduino Due only USB Programming port, or 7-12 V on VIN

g.text: PS3 -> the light only 5 V, >= 2 A regulated

g.desc: PS3 -> the light only 5 V, >= 2 A regulated

g.text: Three failures this rules out:

g.desc: Three failures this rules out:

g.text: * POWERING THE PI FROM THE DUE'S +5V PIN. That pin is the USB bus rail,

g.desc: * POWERING THE PI FROM THE DUE'S +5V PIN. That pin is the USB bus rail,

g.text: fused upstream at 500 mA. A Pi 3B+ idles near 400 mA and peaks well past

g.desc: fused upstream at 500 mA. A Pi 3B+ idles near 400 mA and peaks well past

g.text: 1 A. The Pi browns out, corrupts its SD card, and drags the Due down with

g.desc: 1 A. The Pi browns out, corrupts its SD card, and drags the Due down with

g.text: it - and the symptom looks like a firmware bug for a long time.

g.desc: it - and the symptom looks like a firmware bug for a long time.

g.text: * POWERING THE LIGHT FROM EITHER BOARD. A 1 A load pulled through a board's

g.desc: * POWERING THE LIGHT FROM EITHER BOARD. A 1 A load pulled through a board's

g.text: 5 V pin and its ground plane puts the load's switching current into the

g.desc: 5 V pin and its ground plane puts the load's switching current into the

g.text: same copper the UART uses as its reference. See the ground rules below.

g.desc: same copper the UART uses as its reference. See the ground rules below.

g.text: * BACK-FEEDING. Tying two 5 V rails together means two regulators fighting

g.desc: * BACK-FEEDING. Tying two 5 V rails together means two regulators fighting

g.text: over one node, with current flowing backwards into whichever is lower.

g.desc: over one node, with current flowing backwards into whichever is lower.

g.text: Neither supply is designed for it and neither has reverse protection.

g.desc: Neither supply is designed for it and neither has reverse protection.

g.text: The +5V pin on the Due's header is drawn on these sheets, unconnected and

g.desc: The +5V pin on the Due's header is drawn on these sheets, unconnected and

g.text: marked, only so that you can see it is deliberately not used.

g.desc: marked, only so that you can see it is deliberately not used.

g.text: THE HONEST CAVEAT - USB MAY HAVE ALREADY TIED YOUR GROUNDS

g.desc: THE HONEST CAVEAT - USB MAY HAVE ALREADY TIED YOUR GROUNDS

g.text: If the Due and the Pi are both plugged into the same host computer, that host has bonded their grounds through its own

g.desc: If the Due and the Pi are both plugged into the same host computer, that host has bonded their grounds through its own

g.text: chassis, and legs 2 and 3 now form a loop with it whether you drew one or not.

g.desc: chassis, and legs 2 and 3 now form a loop with it whether you drew one or not.

g.text: This is tolerable and not worth designing around: the loop carries no significant current, because the load return does

g.desc: This is tolerable and not worth designing around: the loop carries no significant current, because the load return does

g.text: not pass through it. What matters is unchanged - the 1 A path is PS3(+) -> LA1 -> Q1 -> PS3(-), and nothing else shares it.

g.desc: not pass through it. What matters is unchanged - the 1 A path is PS3(+) -> LA1 -> Q1 -> PS3(-), and nothing else shares it.

g.text: If you want the drawn topology to be the real one, power the Due from its barrel jack rather than from the same host as

g.desc: If you want the drawn topology to be the real one, power the Due from its barrel jack rather than from the same host as

g.text: the Pi.

g.desc: the Pi.

g.text: PS1(-) and PS2(-) are not star legs: each supply's return is

g.desc: PS1(-) and PS2(-) are not star legs: each supply's return is

g.text: inside its own power cable and is that board's ground already.

g.desc: inside its own power cable and is that board's ground already.

g.text: leg 3 PS3(-) -> Pi GND, J8 pin 14, microamps

g.desc: leg 3 PS3(-) -> Pi GND, J8 pin 14, microamps

g.text: POWER AND GROUND PLAN

g.desc: POWER AND GROUND PLAN

g.text: leg 1 PS3(-) -> Q1 source, sheet 3, up to 1 A

g.desc: leg 1 PS3(-) -> Q1 source, sheet 3, up to 1 A

g.text: to LA1 and Q1, sheet 3

g.desc: to LA1 and Q1, sheet 3

g.text: STAR GROUND

g.desc: STAR GROUND

g.text: leg 2 PS3(-) -> Due GND, LHS pos 18, microamps

g.desc: leg 2 PS3(-) -> Due GND, LHS pos 18, microamps

g.text: the Due's +5V header pin feeds nothing here - deliberately

g.desc: the Due's +5V header pin feeds nothing here - deliberately
